title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Mercimek Corbasi: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with an authentic Mercimek Corbasi recipe! This easy Turkish red lentil soup is hearty, flavorful, and perfect for a cozy meal. Get the step-by-step guide.
longTailKeywords: easy Turkish red lentil soup recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: Turkish red lentil soup with mint
seo.h1: Authentic Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)
h2Headings: Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of warm, authentic Mercimek Corbasi, Turkish red lentil soup, garnished with fresh mint.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich flavors of Mercimek Corbasi, a comforting Turkish red lentil soup perfect for any season.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and comforting flavors. This traditional dish is simple yet incredibly satisfying, often enjoyed as a starter or a light meal. Its smooth texture and aromatic spices make it a staple in every Turkish home.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetables and Lentils
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Finely chop the onion, carrot, and potato (if using). This preparation ensures a smooth soup texture.

instructions.title: Sauté the Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 3-5 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the chopped carrot and potato, and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Lentils
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste, cumin powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and deepen the flavor of the tomato paste. Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot and stir to combine with the vegetables.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend to Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you dont have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Butter Topping and Serve
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber) and cook for 30 seconds, just until fragrant and the butter is slightly browned. Do not burn the spices. Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the spiced butter over each serving and garnish with fresh lemon wedges. Serve immediately.

tips: Ensure the butter and red pepper flakes are gently heated for the terbiye (topping) to develop its full aroma without burning. This is crucial for the authentic flavor.
For an even richer flavor, use chicken or vegetable broth instead of just water. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at serving is essential for authenticity and brightens the flavors.
Mercimek Corbasi can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days and reheats beautifully. It often tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld together.
article:
Turkish cuisine is a tapestry woven with rich flavors, ancient traditions, and a profound sense of hospitality. From sizzling kebabs to sweet baklava, each dish tells a story of cultural heritage and culinary artistry. Among the myriad of delights, one dish stands out for its comforting warmth, nourishing properties, and widespread appeal: Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This vibrant, creamy soup is a staple in every Turkish home and restaurant, offering a taste of home and a hug in a bowl.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi?

Mercimek Corbasi (pronounced mer-jee-MEK chor-BAH-suh) is more than just a soup; it's an institution in Turkey. This creamy, bright orange-red lentil soup is a testament to how simple ingredients can yield an incredibly profound and satisfying flavor. Made primarily from red lentils, onions, garlic, and tomato paste, it's often brightened with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and finished with a fragrant drizzle of melted butter infused with dried mint and Aleppo pepper (pul biber).

Its popularity stems from its accessibility, affordability, and the sheer comfort it provides. Whether it's served as a hearty breakfast, a light lunch, a starter to a grand meal, or a late-night snack, Mercimek Corbasi fits every occasion. It’s known for its smooth texture, achieved by blending the cooked lentils, and its balanced flavor profile – savory, slightly tangy, with a subtle warmth from the spices. Historically, lentils have been a vital food source in Anatolia for centuries, making this soup a timeless classic that continues to nourish and delight generations.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its humble yet powerful ingredient list. Each component plays a crucial role in building the soup's distinctive flavor and texture.

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. They cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture and vibrant color. Be sure to rinse them thoroughly before cooking.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Sautéing these until softened releases their sweet and savory notes, forming the base of the soup's flavor.
*   **Tomato Paste:** Adds depth of umami, a touch of sweetness, and enhances the red hue.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** Used for sautéing the aromatics and as a rich finishing drizzle.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** The liquid base. Broth will add extra depth of flavor.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** Essential seasonings to bring out all the flavors.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** A signature Turkish herb that adds a refreshing, aromatic counterpoint to the richness of the lentils.
*   **Aleppo Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** Provides a mild, fruity heat and a beautiful red color to the finishing drizzle.
*   **Fresh Lemon:** Crucial for brightening the soup with its tangy acidity, cutting through the richness and enhancing all the other flavors.

## How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step

Making delicious Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process that yields incredibly rewarding results. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Start by thoroughly rinsing 1 cup of red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, ensuring a cleaner flavor. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
3.  **Build the Base:** Stir in 1 tablespoon of tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly. This step helps to deepen the flavor of the tomato paste by caramelizing it slightly.
4.  **Add Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring them into the aromatic mixture. Pour in 6 cups of water or vegetable broth. Season with 1 teaspoon of salt and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper.
5.  **Simmer:** Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend to Perfection:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender to carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
7.  **Prepare the Finishing Drizzle (Nane Yağı):** In a small pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add 1 teaspoon of dried mint and 1/2 teaspoon of Aleppo pepper flakes (pul biber). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the mint or pepper.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with the spiced butter mixture. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing.

## Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving that perfect, authentic Mercimek Corbasi is easy with a few simple tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils:** Don't skip this step! Rinsing removes starches that can make the soup cloudy and improves the overall texture and taste.
*   **Quality Tomato Paste:** A good quality tomato paste makes a noticeable difference in flavor. Cooking it briefly before adding liquid helps to deepen its sweetness and umami.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Butter Drizzle:** The *nane yağı* (mint and pepper infused butter) is not just a garnish; it's an integral part of the Mercimek Corbasi experience, adding rich flavor and aroma. Use real butter for the best result.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the end brightens the entire soup, balancing its richness and bringing out all the subtle flavors.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** If your soup is too thick after blending, thin it out with a little hot water or broth until it reaches your desired creamy, pourable consistency.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, or lemon as needed. Flavors can change during cooking.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. It’s often served as a starter, but its hearty nature also makes it suitable as a light main course.

*   **Garnishes:** Beyond the essential mint and pul biber butter, you can also sprinkle a little extra dried mint, a swirl of good quality olive oil, or a pinch of sumac for an extra tangy note.
*   **Bread:** A warm, crusty piece of Turkish pide bread or any fresh crusty bread is the perfect accompaniment for dipping and soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Salad:** A simple Turkish shepherd's salad (Çoban Salata) with finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and peppers, dressed with olive oil and lemon, offers a refreshing contrast.
*   **Pickles:** Turkish pickles (turşu) are a common pairing, providing a delightful sour crunch that complements the soup's richness.
*   **Yogurt:** While not traditional for Mercimek Corbasi, a dollop of plain yogurt can add a creamy, cooling element, especially if you prefer a milder spice level.

## Variations & Dietary Adaptations

One of the great things about Mercimek Corbasi is how easily it adapts to various dietary needs and preferences without sacrificing flavor.

*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** This soup is naturally vegan if you use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing the aromatics and for the finishing drizzle. Ensure your broth is vegetable-based.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Mercimek Corbasi is inherently gluten-free, making it a safe and delicious option for those with gluten sensitivities.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Aleppo pepper flakes in the finishing drizzle to your preference. For more heat, you can add a pinch of regular red pepper flakes. For less, omit them or use a tiny amount.
*   **Added Vegetables:** While the traditional recipe is quite simple, you can add finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the lentils for extra nutrition and body. Add them at the same time as the lentils to ensure they cook through.
*   **Broth Choice:** While water is often used, using vegetable broth will deepen the savory notes of the soup. Chicken broth can also be used if you're not keeping it vegetarian/vegan.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a dish; it's a culinary hug, a taste of Turkish hospitality, and a testament to the power of simple, wholesome ingredients. Learning to make this iconic soup brings a piece of Turkish warmth into your kitchen and promises a comforting, flavorful experience every time. So go ahead, gather your ingredients, and prepare to be enchanted by the timeless allure of this magnificent red lentil soup.
